name: Pont de Roussanes — loop from Clairac
name_original: Pont de Roussanes — boucle au départ de Clairac
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Clairac are characterized by the Lot River valley and its rich agricultural landscapes. The region features extensive vineyards, fruit orchards, and sunflower fields, providing varied scenery for cyclists. It serves as a connection point to major cycling routes like the Lot Valley Cycle Route and the Canal des 2 Mers Cycle Route, offering both riverside paths and routes through rolling countryside. The terrain generally includes gentle gradients along waterways and more challenging sections with elevation gains away from the rivers. Best touring cycling routes around Clairac The most popular touring cycling route is Pont de Roussanes — loop from Clairac, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes about 47 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Lot River, passing by the historic Roussannes bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lot Valley cycle route – View of Castelmoron-sur-Lot loop from Clairac, an easy 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Lot Valley and agricultural landscapes, including plum and strawberry fields. Local touring cyclists also love the The impressive Chêne de Tombeboeuf in Guyenne, a 43.7 miles (70.3 km) trail leading through the countryside of Guyenne, often completed in about 4 hours 36 minutes. Touring cycling around Clairac is defined by the Lot River, agricultural fields, and connections to long-distance cycle routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Clairac are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 1600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Clairac's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Clairac?
A: There are over 140 touring cycling routes around Clairac listed on komoot, offering a wide variety of options for exploration. These routes cater to different skill levels, with 34 easy, 67 moderate, and 39 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Clairac?
A: Clairac, often called the 'Tuscany of France,' offers diverse and picturesque landscapes for touring cyclists. You'll primarily cycle through the Lot River valley, encountering extensive vineyards, fruit orchards (especially plum and strawberry fields), and vibrant sunflower fields. The region's rich alluvial soil contributes to this varied agricultural scenery, complemented by historical bridges and charming villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Clairac?
A: Yes, Clairac offers several easy routes suitable for families. The region's generally gentle gradients along waterways make for pleasant rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287036193/pont-de-roussanes-loop-from-clairac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Roussanes — loop from Clairac&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.3 km trail that follows the Lot River, passing by the historic Roussannes bridge, perfect for a family outing.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Clairac, you can discover several interesting landmarks. The region is rich in historical sites, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4944373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Clairac&lt;/a&gt; with its Benedictine abbey and charming half-timbered houses. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, historical bridges like the Roussannes bridge, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buzet-sur-Baïse Lock&lt;/a&gt;. The Lot River itself, with its sandy beach near Clairac, is a central feature.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Clairac?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Clairac are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41479700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lot Valley cycle route – View of Castelmoron-sur-Lot loop from Clairac&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25 km path offering scenic views of the Lot Valley and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Clairac?
A: The region around Clairac benefits from a pleasant climate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, the weather is generally mild, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful, with blooming orchards in spring and rich harvest colors in autumn. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along shaded river paths, but it can get quite warm.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Clairac?
A: The touring cycling routes in Clairac are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 290 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque agricultural landscapes, the peaceful riverside paths along the Lot River, and the opportunity to connect with major long-distance routes like the Lot Valley Cycle Route and the Canal des 2 Mers Cycle Route.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, while many routes follow gentle river paths, Clairac also offers more challenging options with significant elevation gains away from the rivers. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2092819738/the-impressive-chene-de-tombeboeuf-in-guyenne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The impressive Chêne de Tombeboeuf in Guyenne&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70.3 km trail that leads through the rolling countryside, providing a more strenuous ride for advanced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Clairac using public transport?
A: Clairac is a smaller town, and while specific public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited, it serves as a crucial link to major cycling routes. For detailed information on public transport access with bikes, especially for connections to the wider Lot Valley Cycle Route or Canal des 2 Mers Cycle Route, it's advisable to check local transport schedules or consult the official France Vélo Tourisme website for regional connections: &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/lot-valley-cycle-route/canal-des-2-mers-clairac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lot Valley Cycle Route - Canal des 2 Mers Clairac&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region around Clairac is dotted with charming villages and agricultural areas, offering opportunities for refreshment. Clairac itself has dining options, including near the sandy beach by the abbey. As you cycle through the countryside, you'll find local cafes and small shops in towns like Castelmoron-sur-Lot or other villages along the Lot River, perfect for a break and to sample local produce.

Q: What is the terrain like on the touring cycling routes around Clairac?
A: The terrain around Clairac is varied. Along the Lot River and its tributaries, you'll find mostly flat or gently undulating paths, often paved or well-maintained. Away from the waterways, the routes can become more rolling, with significant elevation gains as you traverse the agricultural countryside and vineyards. This mix offers options for both leisurely rides and more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any specific long-distance cycling routes that connect through Clairac?
A: Yes, Clairac is strategically located as a connection point for significant long-distance cycling routes. It links to both the Lot Valley Cycle Route (V86), which follows the Lot River, and the Canal des 2 Mers Cycle Route, which connects the Garonne to the Mediterranean Sea. These connections provide extensive touring opportunities beyond the immediate vicinity of Clairac.
tips: Like many small towns located along the Lot River, Clairac has its own beach. But it also boasts some of the region's most beautiful half-timbered houses, as well as the Saint-Pierre-ès-Liens church, known for its remarkable maître d'hôtel.
The Lot Valley Véloroute connects Cahors (Lot) to Aiguillon (Lot-et-Garonne), following the Lot river through vineyards, orchards and preserved landscapes.

Although there were many at the end of the 19th and beginning of the 20th century, there are only 5 suspension bridges still in operation between Lot and Garonne. Among them, a few are remarkable. Like the Roussannes bridge, over the Lot, between Granges-sur-Lot and Lafitte-sur-Lot. The work dates from 1841 and was restored by the Lot-et-Garonne departmental council in 2009.
